name: Regin
description: Regin is a skilled smith and dwarf from Norse mythology, best known as the foster father and mentor to the hero Sigurd. He is the son of Hreidmarr and brother to Fafnir and Ótr. Regin's defining characteristic is his cunning and greed, which ultimately leads to his downfall. His most significant role lies in manipulating Sigurd into slaying Fafnir, who had transformed into a dragon to guard the cursed treasure Andvari's hoard. Regin crafts the mighty sword Gram for Sigurd, knowing that it is the only weapon capable of piercing Fafnir's hide. After Sigurd kills the dragon, Regin instructs him to roast Fafnir's heart, planning to eat it himself and gain the dragon's power. However, Sigurd tastes the heart and gains the ability to understand birdsong, learning of Regin's treachery. Forewarned, Sigurd beheads Regin, claiming the treasure for himself and fulfilling his destiny. Regin's story serves as a cautionary tale about greed and the corrupting influence of power, while also highlighting the importance of wisdom and understanding in the face of deceit. He is a pivotal figure in the Volsunga Saga, shaping the events that lead to the tragic downfall of the Volsung clan.
subtitle: Cunning Smith, Mentor, and Betrayer

keyMyths.title: The Slaying of Fafnir
keyMyths.description: Regin manipulates Sigurd into slaying his brother Fafnir, who guards a vast treasure. He forges the sword Gram for Sigurd to use in the battle.

keyMyths.title: Regin's Treachery
keyMyths.description: After Sigurd kills Fafnir, Regin plots to kill Sigurd and take the treasure for himself. However, Sigurd learns of the betrayal through birdsong.

keyMyths.title: Regin's Death
keyMyths.description: Sigurd, warned by the birds, beheads Regin before Regin can enact his treacherous plan. Sigurd then claims the treasure for himself.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: Primary source for Regin's story and his role in Sigurd's life.
sources.text: Primary source for Regin's story and his role in Sigurd's life.
